speaker: DOCTOR
text: Good afternoon, Ms. Smith. Tell me what brought you in today.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've had this pain in my belly since last night. It's getting worse.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Where exactly is the pain? Can you point to it?

speaker: PATIENT
text: It started around my belly button, but now it's more on the lower right side.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: On a scale of one to ten, how bad is it right now?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Maybe a seven. It's sharp, and it hurts more when I move or cough.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I threw up once this morning. I feel warm but I didn't check my temperature.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said it started last night — what time exactly?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, thinking back, it was more like yesterday afternoon, around three. Not last night.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Understood. Any change in appetite or bowel movements?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I haven't wanted to eat anything. No diarrhea.

speaker: PATIENT
text: Just ibuprofen for headaches, and a multivitamin. Nothing prescription.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I'm going to examine your abdomen now. I'll press gently; tell me where it hurts most.
